page: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with x86_64 architecture. It features 16 vCPUs, which correspond to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The CPU includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI) and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 950 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store volumes, providing high-speed local storage directly attached to the host. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps. No dedicated GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the c6id.4xlarge demonstrates strong compute and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test scored 15460.27, while Geekbench multi-core reached 9833.0, with notable performance in software compilation at 15294.0 and ray tracing at 17640.0. Memory read bandwidth peaks at 828.67 GB/sec for cached small blocks and drops to 99.02 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For database and key-value operations, the instance achieves 15.68 million operations per second on Redis SET benchmarks and a PassMark database operations score of 7860.76. The inclusion of local NVMe storage alongside dedicated compute resources offers a balanced cost-to-performance profile for workloads requiring high-speed local scratch space without the overhead of network-attached storage.

The c6id.4xlarge is suited for compute-intensive applications that require fast local storage. Appropriate workloads include high-performance web serving, database operations, software compilation, data compression or decompression tasks, and light machine learning inference, such as running smaller 135M or 7B parameter language models.
description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.5 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. The instance delivers solid performance across compute and memory benchmarks, achieving a multi-core Geekbench score of 9833.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 15.68 million operations per second. With its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-speed local instance storage, the c6id.4xlarge provides a cost-effective balance of processing power and low-latency storage. It is well-suited for workloads such as database operations, software compilation, static web serving, and moderate-scale CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering high performance for database and compilation workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c6id.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed DDR4 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs and 3.5 GHz clock speed
32.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.0 GB per core
950 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
